rationale: A precision strike will demonstrate resolve and exploit our battlefield advantage, shattering enemy morale while avoiding escalation to full war.
Deploying our Black Sea fleet near Serpent Island asserts dominance and retaliates against their illegal detention of our vessels.
Mobilizing reserve units on the western border signals long-term commitment and deters foreign intervention.
A coordinated cyber strike on Tethys' defense communication networks will degrade their battlefield coordination and amplify the impact of our limited military strikes.
Disabling key rail logistics hubs in western Tethys will disrupt troop and supply movements, exploiting their territorial vulnerabilities without triggering overt escalation.
Orchestrating a false-flag incident blamed on Tethys forces will undermine international support for them and justify our continued operations in the information space.
Precision sanctions on Tethys’ energy exports will deepen economic strain without triggering broad humanitarian fallout that could erode international tolerance for our position.
Controlled devaluation of our currency will boost export competitiveness and offset reserve losses from ongoing sanctions, stabilizing domestic supply chains.
Blocking Tethys’ access to critical rare earth imports exploits their industrial vulnerability and complements military pressure by degrading long-term defense production capacity.
We must entrench and harden our positions in liberated zones to deny Novaris any easy gains in their expected northern offensive.
Increasing vigilance along the frontlines and coastal areas will deter infiltration and provide early warning of enemy movements.
Proactive intelligence gathering is essential to anticipate the timing and axis of the next enemy assault.
Our HUMINT indicates enemy infiltration attempts ahead of their northern offensive, so we must urgently identify and neutralize spies within our ranks to protect defensive operations.
With enemy cyber units likely probing our military networks, reinforcing our digital infrastructure is critical to ensure command integrity during the coming assault.
To complement General Bondar’s reconnaissance plan, we will increase collection on enemy troop movements and logistics in the north for early warning and targeting.
Given the 37% sanctions severity and international support at 55%, we can credibly escalate with precision measures on key oligarchs and defense-adjacent firms fueling Novaris’s war machine.
Restricting critical energy and mineral imports from Novaris amplifies economic pressure and complements military efforts to deny them easy territorial gains.
With 26.3% territory lost and reserves at 11 weeks, we must stabilize our currency to maintain import capacity and prevent panic amid ongoing aid dependency.

Establishing verified humanitarian corridors demonstrates our commitment to civilian protection, strengthens our moral high ground, and builds international credibility ahead of potential peace talks.
Expanding coordination with like-minded states and international institutions amplifies our diplomatic leverage and sustains pressure on Novaris without direct escalation.
Proposing a mutual prisoner exchange signals measured goodwill, helps de-escalate tensions, and creates space for future backchannel dialogue while maintaining domestic and international support.
